name: Los Hornachos and Hinojosa del Valle — circular through the towns of the Campiña Extremeña
name_original: Los Hornachos y Hinojosa del Valle — circular por los pueblos de la Campiña Extremeña
region_intro: Road cycling around Ribera Del Fresno features routes through the Campiña Extremeña, characterized by rolling agricultural landscapes and dehesa woodlands. The terrain includes moderate ascents and descents, connecting rural towns and offering views of the Extremaduran countryside. Cyclists can expect varied surfaces typical of regional roads, suitable for road bikes. The area provides a network of paths that traverse open plains and gentle hills. Best road cycling routes around Ribera Del Fresno The most popular road cycling route is Los Hornachos and Hinojosa del Valle — circular through the towns of the Campiña Extremeña, a 49.5 miles (79.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 42 minutes to complete. This route connects several towns within the Campiña Extremeña. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Villafranca de los Barros – El Capricho de Cotrina loop from Villafranca de los Barros, a difficult 46.1 miles (74.2 km) path. This loop offers significant elevation changes through the rural landscape. Local road cyclists also love the El Capricho de Cotrina – Fuente del Maestre loop from Ribera del Fresno, a 35.8 miles (57.6 km) trail leading through agricultural areas and past local landmarks, often completed in about 2 hours 27 minutes. Road cycling around Ribera Del Fresno is defined by rolling hills, agricultural plains, and dehesa woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging routes with greater elevation gain. The routes in Ribera Del Fresno are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 260 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Ribera Del Fresno's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for road cycling routes in Ribera Del Fresno?
A: Road cycling routes around Ribera Del Fresno offer a range of difficulties. You'll find routes categorized as easy, moderate, and difficult, catering to various experience levels. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villafranca de los Barros loop from Villafranca de los Barros&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27631059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villafranca de los Barros – El Capricho de Cotrina loop&lt;/a&gt; is considered difficult due to significant elevation changes.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Ribera Del Fresno area?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes available around Ribera Del Fresno, offering a wide variety of options for exploring the Campiña Extremeña.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Ribera Del Fresno?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes perfect for beginners. One example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villafranca de los Barros loop from Villafranca de los Barros&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a gentler introduction to the region's cycling. In total, there are 7 easy routes to choose from.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Ribera Del Fresno?
A: The best time for road cycling in Ribera Del Fresno is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for longer rides, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Ribera Del Fresno?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Ribera Del Fresno are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992859414/los-hornachos-and-hinojosa-del-valle-circular-through-the-towns-of-the-campina-extremena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Hornachos and Hinojosa del Valle — circular through the towns of the Campiña Extremeña&lt;/a&gt;, which connects several local towns.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling in Ribera Del Fresno?
A: You can expect to cycle through diverse landscapes characteristic of the Campiña Extremeña, including rolling agricultural plains, traditional dehesa woodlands, and gentle hills. Routes often connect rural towns, offering views of the Extremaduran countryside.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes pass by notable attractions. For example, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4270417" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Capricho de Cotrina&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4082806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vineyards of Ribera del Guadiana&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also offer scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4082804" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;views of Los Santos de Maimona&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where do road cycling routes typically start in Ribera Del Fresno?
A: Many road cycling routes in the area start from towns like Ribera del Fresno itself, or nearby Villafranca de los Barros. These towns often serve as convenient hubs with amenities and potential parking options.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Ribera Del Fresno?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, the quiet roads connecting charming towns, and the scenic views of the Extremaduran countryside.

Q: Are there long-distance road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, experienced riders will find challenging long-distance routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40640476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa Extremeña – Views of Feria loop from Villafranca de los Barros&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a difficult route spanning over 135 km with significant elevation gain, perfect for a full day's ride.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes connect various towns and villages within the Campiña Extremeña, such as Los Hornachos, Hinojosa del Valle, and Fuente del Maestre. These towns typically offer local cafes, bars, and restaurants where you can stop for refreshments and meals.

Q: What is the average duration for road cycling routes in Ribera Del Fresno?
A: The duration of road cycling routes varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. Shorter, easy routes might take around 1.5 to 2 hours, while longer, more challenging routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40640476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa Extremeña – Views of Feria loop&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 6 hours to complete.
tips: Hinojosa del Valle is a small rural town on the Badajoz plain. Surrounded by fields of cereals and sheep farming, the fertile grounds of this valley are home to fantastic gravel trails.
Typical landscape of Dehesas, especially beautiful in springtime where meadows and green trees abound among oaks.

The town of Hornachos rises on the same mountain range that carries the snow up to the highest point, the Iglesia de la Purísima Concepción. From this point you can see the entire population and landscape in the background and above, the castle that bears the same name as the nucleus, on the massif.
